title: Review: identity/hpke/bus/main/daemon/ffi/tui round 26
notes:
Maintainer risk: HIGH — FFI client ships without E2E encryption and without proxy support (two P1 items that affect the public API surface). The bus Redis silent-drop is P1 correctness for any multi-instance deployment. The OSC unbounded escape swallow is an exploitable display attack from any relay-authenticated peer.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: Redis bus code path is feature-gated and untestable without a Redis instance; the room HPKE AAD protocol is only documented in TUI source (not DESIGN.md), so other clients may already be using a different convention that I cannot check without reading all clients simultaneously; the OSC-no-cap finding depends on the render batch assumption (multiple messages per draw call) which I verified in draw_chat but not for the EventStream path. Would raise confidence if: integration test exercising Redis bus publish/subscribe round-trip; DESIGN.md specifying the room HPKE AAD format; cross-client E2E test (TUI + FFI + wasm) for sealed_broadcast.
